monsieur buzzy is back inside my ear today (it turns out he took some time off yesterday). unfortunately, he feels invigorated. you know, in another life - he smacks his lips - you could have really been something. my stomach sinks. in another life, people would have been proud of you. and there was a time when you had the chance to become anything: a psychiatrist...a lawyer....an artist...a nurse....a writer...honestly, anything would have been better than this, he motions to me. 

but i like this! i tell him. i am happy now. i am proud of how far i've come. i am thankful the universe gave me a second chance and i will make the most of this. i can still help people...i will still make a positive impact on this world. 

we both know that's a lie, he sneers. we both know that you are just barely surviving. he rubs his hands together. i bet business school was never your first choice. you wouldn't have gotten burnt out if you were doing something you loved. in fact - he pauses for dramatic effect - it's time for you to own up to all of this. 

what do you mean? i already know what he means. i ask because i know monsieur buzzy wants me to ask and not because i want to hear what he has to say.

it's your own fault. he pauses again to shift into a more comfortable position. my hands are shaking now. you could have fought harder, but you gave up too early. and giving up was a conscious decision that you made. i bet you feel guilty because deep down you know it's true. i can barely hear him now - his voice sounds so far away. 

i'm trying my best, i sob. i'm trying my best, but sometimes i wish i had just died.

monsieur buzzy nods. that might have been less despicable, and yet you couldn't even manage that. instead, you missed the one opportunity you had to redeem yourself. monsieur buzzy is heartless. it was selfish of you to prioritize your own pain. you ran and you hid and because of that, you have most likely ruined the lives of countless other people. 

stop talking, i beg. please go and bother someone else. 

you will never ever get rid of me, monsieur buzzy hisses. never. there will be days when you'll think i'm gone for good, but i will always, always come back.

it's 3 am in my bedroom and i am staring at my reflection in the full-length mirror. my entire body looks distorted - it's like i am looking into a funhouse mirror. i can tell that ghost is nearby. i know you're in there....come out i whisper (because charles is asleep). 

boo! says ghost.

monsieur buzzy was in my ear all day yesterday, whispering crazy things. monsieur buzzy is a pessimist and a radical skeptic - he says things like nothing really matters and there is no inherent value, meaning or order to life. any meaning created by human beings - love, joy, freedom, or faith - is just a fiction used as a coping strategy while we sit and wait for death. monsieur buzzy has a whiny voice and spits a little whenever he talks, even when whispering. monsieur buzzy does not believe in purpose and has the propensity to be very manipulative. while walking home from work, i told him i was afraid because everything felt surreal - the whole world felt distant, distorted, and empty. the crushing weight of hopelessness made it too difficult for me to walk. you should destroy everything monsieur buzzy replied while smacking his lips inside my ear - it was very disgusting. he had just woken up from a nap. your entire reality is just a subjective construct of human perception anyways. honestly, you should just end it. he always says things like this when he knows i am vulnerable.

monsieur buzzy is out and about bothering somebody else today. i am relieved. i am trying my hardest to live lightly.

“It’s dark because you’re trying too hard,” said Susila. “Dark because you want it to be light. Remember what you used to tell me when I was a little girl. ‘Lightly, child, lightly. You’ve got to learn to do everything lightly.
Think lightly, act lightly, feel lightly. Yes, feel lightly, even though you’re feeling deeply. Just lightly let things happen and lightly cope with them.’ I was so preposterously serious in those days, such a humorless little prig. Lightly, lightly — it was the best advice ever given me.
​
So throw away all your baggage and go forward. There are quicksands all about you, sucking at your feet, trying to suck you down into fear and self-pity and despair. That’s why you must walk so lightly. Lightly, my darling. On tiptoes; and no luggage, not even a sponge bag. Completely unencumbered.” ​
​
​​
— From Island by Aldous Huxley.​

text: It's been proven that time feels like it goes by faster the older you are, but I wish things would slow down sometimes. I wonder if life speeds up more if you see everything as mundane instead of living with intentionality. Maybe time flies when you're going through the motions and nothing feels new. I wonder if time would decelerate if we started seeing everything as if for the first time.
I really want to be able to wish you well one day.
Living your life to the fullest is an unsolvable problem.

I spend a lot of time wondering what my life might be like if I were braver, wiser, or less afraid of the world around me. Sometimes I spiral into nihilism because the gap between who I am and who I want to be is so tragically great and ever-widening. I am currently trying to figure out what it would mean for me to "meet myself where I am"; I am starting to realize that my personal fears, trauma, and cynicism are not going anywhere soon - regardless of how hard I pretend they don't exist. I wonder if my tendency to hyperfixate on the person I wish was has stunted my personal growth and healing. In the desperate effort to avoid pain, failure, or guilt, I may have both caused and exacerbated all of these symptoms. Maybe it would be healthier if I told myself that my best was good enough and stopped beating myself up for being imperfect. Pain, stress, and the feeling of discomfort are all reminders of our humanity and our aptitude for growth and adaptation. Our ability to respond to negative feedback reflects our innate human capacity to be resilient. Also, mistakes grow your brain by creating more synapses (if you learn from them). So maybe not everything imperfect is bad.

Above all, do not lie to yourself. A man who lies to himself and listens to his own lie comes to a point where he does not discern any truth either in himself or anywhere around him, and thus falls into disrespect towards himself and others. Not respecting anyone, he ceases to love, and having no love, he gives himself up to passions and coarse pleasures, in order to occupy and amuse himself, and in his vices reaches complete bestiality, and it all comes from lying continually to others and to himself. A man who lies to himself is often the first to take offense. It sometimes feels very good to take offense, doesn’t it? And surely he knows that no one has offended him, and that he himself has invented the offense and told lies just for the beauty of it, that he has exaggerated for the sake of effect, that he has picked on a word and made a mountain out of a pea—he knows all of that, and still he is the first to take offense, he likes feeling offended, it gives him great pleasure, and thus he reaches the point of real hostility.

— Fyodor Dostoevsky. The Brothers Karamazov.

Man is condemned to be free. Condemned because he did not create himself, yet is nevertheless at liberty, and from the moment he is thrown into this world he is responsible for everything he does.

- Jean-Paul Sartre

I think plants are beautiful reflections of homeostasis and diversity living in harmony; they have the ability to maintain stable internal states while balancing numerous conflicting external demands. They also communicate with each other and their environment through underground fungi networks. These networks make the entire community stronger as the fungi deliver messages of danger from plant to plant and can even send nutrients to other plants that need them. By observing plants, I've come to appreciate their quiet transformations and unique self-expression. I've also been fascinated by the ways they naturally cycle through periods of rest and regrowth each season. Flowers don't force themselves to blossom during extreme or stressful conditions and plants don't continue to photosynthesize in harsh conditions. In fact, periods of rest are vital - this is the time to accumulate resources for the blooming season. I think there is a lot to learn from this; the culture of putting work above everything else pressures us to have expectations for ourselves that are unsustainable and unhealthy. If we are not careful, we may start to forget how to self-regulate and start feeling guilty during periods of rest. In our struggle to compete, we may lose our ability to communicate with others within our network and forget how to exist in symbiotic relationships.
And just like that, it no longer feels enough. I’m an emotional black hole and nothing is ever enough.

the idea of being "enough" is an abstraction of the mind. there is no such thing as enough, only what is. my feelings of not being enough were borne out of guilt. i did what i could and what i was capable of at the time. if i could go back i might have done things differently, but i can only move forward and make wiser, kinder decisions as i learn more and grow. perhaps i held on to my guilt for so many years because i was afraid of letting it go, along with my sense of identity. not being enough was always part of me (before i even met you) so it was so easy for my insecurities to latch on and intensify my guilt when our friendship ended. but identity and self are two different things. everything about you that can be put on paper is ego; these things do not define you or determine who you are. and all of these attributes of our identity are also tied to suffering. no matter how hard i try, i will never come close to being perfect. it was wrong for me to have expected myself to be all-knowing, but hopefully i am a little wiser now. 

the people who have committed the greatest evils in the world were the ones who believed they were righteous. we are all easily capable of becoming monsters.
everything is laced with nostalgia these days

only after rejecting all western conceptions of beauty, which teach us to value whiteness and wealth above all else, have i come to appreciate my own identity, wellness, and desirability.

my rest, my ardent dependence on my own care, my own value, my own beauty, will always be dangerous, important, and life-sustaining.
feeling thankful for the people in my life that have asked me to do better because they believed i could; i promise to always try my best

it feels lovely to have a home that is my own; to demonstrate hospitality is such a blessing. the ability to create warmth that can be shared - i remember wanting this for so long and i feel so grateful.
reminder to myself not to freeze people in time. everyone is changing and growing.

teared up because i was really nervous about sending that email.

during the pandemic, i started thinking more about the wellness industry and how it can be very exclusionary (because of cost/accessibility). so much of it is centered around consumerism. wellness is the act of practicing healthy habits on a daily basis to attain better physical and mental health outcomes. we all deserve to thrive, and i believe there are many ways to be the healthiest versions of ourselves that don’t involve spending a lot of money. i really like buying new skincare and wellness products, but i wanted to widen my personal definition of what it means to be well. i think wellness for me right now means working through personal trauma, identifying negative and self-destructive thought patterns, and building healthy long-term habits.

There are countless reasons Harry Potter is the quintessential protagonist, but let’s cut to the top three:
1. He Isn’t Unique
In modern media, protagonists are often impossibly self-reliant, hyper-competent, and uniquely powerful—think Captain Marvel, Wonder Woman, or Superman. But, let’s be real: who watches a Superman movie and thinks, “Yes, that’s me. I too am a flawless, godlike being”? Harry is the “Chosen One,” sure, but not because he’s particularly brilliant, attractive, or even powerful. Honestly, he’s lucky and has two reliable friends, and that’s about it. And this is exactly why Harry works as a protagonist. He’s not some self-insert fantasy character (cough Bella Swan cough), but someone whose weaknesses and ordinariness demand the help of everyone around him.
Harry’s mediocrity pulls us into the Wizarding World because he needs it all—every witch, wizard, house-elf, werewolf, and muggle—to succeed. Without Harry’s inherent vulnerability, the story wouldn’t be nearly as immersive. Unlike flavorless Bella, though, Harry is part of a rich, dynamic world filled with strong personalities who balance his lack of razzle-dazzle. He makes mistakes, struggles, and still chooses what’s right, even when it’s hard. This makes him less of a Superman and more of a flawed, scrappy, deeply human underdog.
2. He’s an Underdog (and We Love Those)
Everyone loves rooting for an underdog. It’s practically baked into our DNA. Harry starts life in the worst way possible—bullied relentlessly by the Dursleys, stuck in a cupboard, and armed only with audacity and stubbornness. Despite being the “Chosen One,” Harry doesn’t waltz into privilege or power. In fact, his so-called “chosen” status is more burden than benefit. It doesn’t make him popular or gifted—just a target.
His peers? Skeptical. The press? Brutal. His professors? Some of them outright hate him. He doesn’t even get cool powers out of the deal. Instead, Harry faces choice after choice that tests his character. He could have taken the easy route—buddying up with Malfoy when invited—but instead he chooses to stand with fellow misfits like Hagrid and Neville. Harry’s underdog status isn’t about being the weakest; it’s about being the one who consistently chooses the harder, nobler path, even when no one’s clapping for him.
3. He Has Serious Personal Issues
Here’s the thing about Harry: he’s not some squeaky-clean hero. He’s got baggage, and a lot of it. He’s moody, impulsive, often jealous, and occasionally downright insufferable. He lashes out, sometimes physically. These aren’t likable traits, but they’re painfully relatable. Harry’s flaws mirror the parts of ourselves we tend to hide—the irrational anger, the unchecked envy, the fear that our messiness makes us unlovable.
But what makes Harry compelling is that he’s not irredeemable. He’s just good enough—brave enough, empathetic enough—that we root for him anyway. His flaws don’t define him; his choices do. Even when his temper jeopardizes friendships, his friends stick around. They forgive him because they see his potential for good. And, as readers, we’re led to do the same. Harry’s imperfections make him vulnerable, but his humanity makes him the kind of protagonist we want to see win.
In the end, Harry Potter isn’t just a character we follow—he’s one we grow with. His ordinariness makes him accessible, his underdog status makes him inspiring, and his flaws make him real. Together, these traits create a hero whose journey isn’t just about defeating evil—it’s about finding strength in imperfection.

Obviously, there are more reasons, but I am tired now.

text: I feel like God brought me through a lot and then was like "k bye" and dropped me off in a pile of shit and now I'm stuck

Staying with my friends at the Bursley dorm instead of my own dorm in Betsy Barbor was a way for me to avoid my fear of being alone.  The idea was to stay with my friends who all live on north campus, until I felt confident enough to go back to my own dorm room on central campus.   The problem is, the longer I stay at Bursley, the more afraid I am of leaving, and the more I dread going to my own dorm room.  


I've been struggling a lot with myself.  Apart from the loneliness, I've also begun to develop a bunch of bad habits that I use to deal with any challenges I face.  I feel like I'm caught in a web of guilt and self hatred, and I'm not sure how to get out of it.  


We were talking about the concept of healing last night in the community group that I recently started going to.  One person asked why are some people unwilling to heal.  I think the answer is that healing is a difficult and sometimes very painful process.  A person who is hurting might not want to get better because they don't want to face this process of getting better.  They might convince themselves that the situation they're in is manageable in order to avoid the idea of potentially suffering more.  


Truth is, I don't like my life right now.  There are a lot of voids that I've been trying to fill with things I know are only temporary.  I think what I really need is a sense of security.

Last night, while cramming for today's biochem test, I made myself listen to as much classical music as possible...just in case it does make you smarter because God knows I need it.

As high school comes to an end (today was the last official day), the feeling is bittersweet.
I am also starting to realize as this chapter of my life comes to an end, how terrified I am, and how my fears can easily affect the direction of my life and decsions.  I think my one biggest fear would have to be the fear of abandonment and the fear of letting go.  In my mind, those two things sum up to one single fear because from past experiences, they often come hand in hand.  I am a people person, and emotional intimacy is something I search for and value highly in relationships, whether it is with a friend, or a significant other.  As someone who grows attached very easily, I have had many mentor figures come into my life, impacting me in a significant way, and then leaving suddenly because of death, moving, their own personal decision, etc. Every goodbye is painful, and memories of people you love and never see anymore hurt a lot as well.  I feel like one of the very worst feelings in the world is the feeling of missing someone.  When I think about certain people who I miss, sometimes I'll cry for them which I think everyone is allowed to do occasionally.  Although those people are no longer part of my life, they have shaped and positively affected me enough for me to miss them this much so it isn't completely a bad thing I guess.

I think my fear of the pain that comes with losing someone close to me has affected the relationships I have with other people today.  I am constantly afraid of being left alone, yet I am constantly afraid of becoming too attached and getting hurt.  It is the most frustrating thing, because my life becomes a constant struggle between being extremely protective and attached to the people I don't want to lose, and pushing the same people away because I don't want to become too vulnerable. This leads to problems such as jealousy, over sensitivity, and overthinking which I know I face all the time.  I think my goal now is just to put myself out there despite the possibility of getting hurt.  I think that is the way to experience more happiness and less regret.

After  being stuck in the car for 7 hours straight and no stops, I've had a lot of time to think.  About things like the fact that I need to pee and how I haven't eaten for 10 hours and my interview in Monday that I don't really know how to prepare for and the fact that I threw up twice from carsickness.  I shouldn't be looking down at this screen, but I'm so bored and there's no one to talk to and no wifi (so I'll copy paste this once we get to the hotel).  These are very dark times kids.  

Oohhhh New York city is breathtaking at night by the way.  
I really want a nice salad and a coke right now.  My parents told me to shut up and stop complaining so here I am, talking to myself.  I bet Beverly and Eunice and Dan are having tons of fun at home doing whatever they want at the moment.  
We visited Fairfield because it was on the way and it looked so depressing and boring.  We drove through miles of nothing, and all of a sudden it was like BAM look it's a school!  I'd much rather go to college in a busier environment.  Like somewhere in a big city.
Ok 23 minutes have passed and still no food.  So this is what starvation feels like.
I'M SO HUNGRY IT HURTS IF SOMEONE OFFERED ME A HANDFUL OF GRASS I WOULD TOTALLY EAT IT 


Now I'm angry.
Where's the food at.
Feed.Me.


I will literally pee on the seats and then smash the windows of this car if no one lets me out.




But I'm too weak from hunger to smash the windows :( 


Ok 40 minutes have passed and still no sign of rescue.  


My feet are cold.
My heart is cold.
I need a tissue for these tears.  


45 minutes have passed.  Is this what dying feels like?


WHY ARE ALL THE RESTAURANTS AND REST STOPS ALWAYS ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF THE HIGHWAY?!?!?!!!!!?????!!?)71?-&2:!:9:9:$:83.@:@/@


I must stop typing now.  I must reserve my energy.  In case I die.


It has been an hour since I first wrote this.  


I might just cry.


WENDY'S!!!!!!


Why isn't there free wifi?!


55 minutes after entering.




We just met a guy who worked at Wendy's.  He says he went to UPenn and  we had a long convo about college because I told him I was visiting schools in Pennsylvania.  I'm so glad to have spoken to a real human being.  He was really nice.  


Now we're back in the goddam car.


It's 10:25 pm.  2 hours and 25 minutes have passed since the start of this post.  
We just checked in at the hotel.  There are two groups of teens at the front entrance.   I am scared.  I think they are drunk.


Groups of teens are one of the scariest things you will ever encounter.  I try my best not to make eye contact.  Be careful because they can literally smell fear.
title: Thoughts during an 8 hour car ride

...and then I remembered why I hadn't asked my mom for money in such a long time.  I've been hearing the same answers since I was like 8, but somehow I forgot..

Me: "Mom, can I have a few dollars to keep in my bag?  You told me you'd reward me for shoveling our abnormally long driveway (we have 2 cars.  this driveway is the length of 8+ cars and is the longest driveway in our neighborhood...)"

Mom: "UHM DID I EVER ASK YOU TO PAY ME BACK FOR RAISING YOU DID I EVER GET PAID TO CLEAN UP AFTER YOU DID I EVER GET PAID TO DO YOUR LAUNDRY I FED YOU I GAVE YOU A HOME I GAVE YOU LIFE"

And then I asked my dad for a ride over the phone today because I had to stay after school to see a teacher.

Me: "Dad, can you pick me up after school at 4:30?  I need to see a teacher and I don't have a ride."

Dad: "CARMELIA HOW MANY TIMES DO I HAVE TO TELL YOU I AM NOT YOUR TAXI DRIVER YOU TAKE TOO MANY THINGS FOR GRANTED JUST LIKE THAT TIME YOU [insert random time I took something for granted 6 years ago] .  PARENTS DO NOT EXIST JUST TO SERVE YOU ETC."

text:
I went tubing last weekend at our church retreat and I know I will never forget that day.  I was wearing my black sweatpants and a long sleeved shirt when I saw the group sitting on Mark's speedboat.  "Wait for me!"  I screamed while I ran down the dock to where they were, waving my arms wildly.  We're going tubing, they explained.  I could come if I wanted to.  Because I was raised in a box, I had never seen anyone go tubing before.  "I'll come!"  I said cheerfully.  I put on a life jacket, got on one of the tube things, and stuck my hands in the straps.  I won't change into shorts or a swimsuit because I'm strong, I told myself.  I have a great grip, and if I just don't let go of these straps, I won't fall off, and if I don't fall off I won't get wet.  5 seconds after the speed boat started moving, we fell off.  The water was ice cold, and my baggy sweatpants started absorbing all the water, getting heavier and heavier.  At that point, I became aware of my lack of upper body strength.  I also began to panic when I realized that my pants were falling off.  "Not at Jesus camp!"  I thought to myself in absolute horror.  At the same time, I was afraid of letting go, knowing that the water would be ice cold and I would probably drown.  Oh yea, and I also don't know how to swim.  "Should I fall off, or should my pants fall off?"  My thoughts were swirling.  I finally let go, deciding that it was not appropriate to let my pants fall off at church camp in front of  half the youth group.  Plunging into the water, I suddenly couldn't breathe and I was helpless, with nothing to grab onto except my own life jacket.  "Hang in there, we're coming back for you!"  My friends yelled across the lake as I watched the boat get smaller and smaller, almost disappearing.  It took these people an hour to come back and get me, I swear.  By the time someone dragged me out of the water (literally...I had no more energy and my fingers weren't working), I was so cold I didn't feel cold.  When someone offered me a towel, I said no thank you I can't feel my face anyways.






Church camp was great.  I learned so much.  Like the fact that the showers can give you hypothermia, and that it really is possible to eat for 24 hours straight.

text:
Small children are terrifying...especially in groups.  I was helping out with the Children's Ministry at my church today, and the room was full of kids racing around and jumping up and down.  I asked a small group of kids if they wanted to play with the plastic food and kitchen utensils, and they said yes so I turned around to grab some plastic plates.  When I turned back around, three little boys and two girls were standing around me, holding plastic forks and knives.  "Uhm, do you want to grab some food?"  I asked, pointing to the basket of little plastic vegetables and meat.  "No," they said, before jumping on top of me and stabbing me repeatedly with the child sized forks and knives.  I yelled, and then my entire face was swallowed by the furry carpet as one of the kids ran over and sat on my head.  "Let's eat her!"  One boy declared.  "No...please don't!"  I said while laughing.  "NO...PLEASE DON'T!!!"  I screamed as two kids bit into my leg.  I honestly don't know how I escaped, but I'm pretty sure I'm missing chunks of hair, and there is now a nice big hole in my shirt.  Children really don't need costumes on Halloween.  If I ever see a group of kids, I will run away screaming at the top of my lungs.

I accidentally got pen marks all over my face and was hiding in one of the stalls, waiting for everyone to leave the bathroom before coming out to wash it off.  Peeking out from the cracks next to the stall door, I accidentally locked eyes with a girl who was fixing her hair in front of the mirror.  For 5 horrifying seconds, we stared at each other through the reflection in the mirror.  DO YOU GUYS REALIZE HOW UNCOMFORTABLE THE SITUATION WAS???? She saw my face pressed against the inside of the stall door and of course it looked like I was spying on her.  I hope she didn't recognize me.  Anyways she laughed awkwardly and quickly left the bathroom.


I mistook some random girl for my friend, and waved wildly from across the lunchroom.  She stared back in confusion, and I suddenly realized that the person I was waving to was a stranger.  Trying to fix the damage, I pretended I was looking past her, and kept smiling, continuing to walk past her.  Problem was, she turned her head to see who I was "actually" waving at, and the only thing behind her was a basically empty table, with two kids talking together, not even facing me...so it was obvious that I wasn't waving at them.  


One time in second grade, I peed my pants in front of the entire class during gym because I had those sweatpants where you tie a knot in the front, and I had just learned how to tie double knots so I did like 5 knots before realizing that I was stuck in my pants.  It was one of the most horrific days of my life, and the other day, I had a dream where I was forced to relive that horrible moment.  Let me tell you, I woke up sweating.  Worst. Nightmare. Ever.  


You know what's worse?  The fact that people who were in my second grade class still remember that incident to this day.  Someone was kind enough to remind me last week.

text:
"I will quickly learn the music in two hours and audition for an Acapella group successfully", I told myself.  WHOOPS.  Everything was fine until the solo part of the audition.  When I opened my mouth, the room was suddenly filled with the sound of screaming llamas.  I didn't know that horrible sound could come from an actual human being.  I always saw those vines online of screaming llamas, but never imagined the same sound coming out of my face.  "Stop!!!!!"  My ears screamed.  "Please, make it stop!!!"  I almost wanted to stop in the middle, and apologize to the poor listeners.  I finally shut my mouth, chuckled nervously, and felt my eye twitching.  "Jesus, take me now", I prayed silently.  I went home traumatized and, frankly, scared of my own voice.

My mom bought hot chocolate, so that was good.  I've been drinking around 3 cups a day.  In fact, I've been replacing water with hot chocolate.  Actually, I don't even remember the last time I drank a glass of plain water.  It was probably months ago.

One time, I discovered the drink V8, which is basically a drink with 2 servings of veggies per can.  I started replacing all my meals with those drinks until my mom got mad and decided to never buy them again.




Remember how I plucked my eyebrows wrong and messed them up?  Well, I'm afraid the damage might be permanent.... D:  It has been about two months I think, and there are still weird gaps.

Today's Likes:
Hot Chocolate
Elementary (great show!)
Perfect weather :)

Today's Dislikes:
Llama vines
The sound of my mom's voice
Tired and stressed about homework and tests and music
title: The Sound of Screaming Llamas

I was wearing athletic clothing last week, and I wanted to show everyone my roundhouse kicks so I could successfully trick them into believing that I'm athletic.  Some random adult walked by, and being the usual hyperactive idiot that I am, I proudly asked him if he wanted to see me do a roundhouse kick.  He said sure, so I did the best roundhouse kick ever, and shook his hand proudly.  "You're not like a dean or something, right?"  I asked him stupidly.  "No", the man replied.  "I'm the associate principal.  What's your name?"  I was so shocked that my eye started twitching and I told him that I couldn't tell him my name.  


My eye is twitching now from the memory.

text:
If you are looking to lower your self-esteem and body image, the school weight room is the place for you.  Scariest moment of my life.  My dear buddy Ben told me that the fitness center was "basically empty" after school, so I was like okay, I guess I'll check it out and maybe work out and it will be a cool judgment free zone where I can feel good about myself.  AHA AHAHA NO.  The moment I walk in there are like 20 guys lifting 1000000 lb weights with red faces that look like >:[ and the next thing you know, I'm screaming internally and slowly backing away as inconspicuously as possible while peeing my pants.  Then I went home and ate comfort food, and Ben messaged me telling me how embarrassing I was and how I didn't even change, etc.


In conclusion, the only thing I gained from this experience was weight.  
























I also have 20 pages of notes left to do for AP Psych, which is actually why I'm on this blog.  Sorry for not posting recently!  Nothing happened because my life is boring and my life basically consists of sleeping eating blogging and laughing at stupid things.  There is of course, the occasional mental breakdown.


Oh yea, 3 days ago, my family was at church and I got in an argument with my dad.  I started talking really loudly, but I swear I wasn’t even yelling!  Anyways, my dad got mad because he said I was raising my voice and showing disrespect.  As punishment, he decided to drive the entire family home when I was still hiding in the bathroom.  I came out and walked around feeling alone and abandoned, while slowly realizing that my entire family was gone and left me there on purpose.
I sat for like, 3 hours in the corner of the church in tears and trying comfort myself while staring at a pamphlet that said, “Life After Loss: Grieving With Hope”. 




Thank goodness Eunice picked up her phone and got one of her college friends to give me a ride back.  


Because you all have been oh so very faithful...here is a gift from me to you:








I now have exactly one hour to do the notes.   I'M IN TROUBLE.  JESUS HELP ME.

HI GUYS!!   So a few days ago, one of my family members threw a shoe at my head. I stood there motionless and watched the shoe fly towards my face in slow motion.  In that moment, time stopped.  My life flashed before my eyes.  I encountered a deep moment of self reflection.  HAHA JUST KIDDING.  I have slow reflexes.  It is sad though, that even after years of martial arts, in that critical moment, I didn't have the ability to block a flying object.  





















So yea, now I have a bloody cut above my left eye where the heel of the shoe clonked me in the face.  Better than a shoe shaped bruise I guess.  Actually, I'm not sure.  

I've been trying to take pictures, because I love looking at pretty pictures on sites like pinterest and tumblr.  I thought to myself, why not?  Photography can't be hard...everyone seems to be into it.  I WAS WRONG.  Out of a total of 86 pictures of STILL LIFE, I got a total of 3 pictures that weren't blurry.  And I suspect that one of those pictures wasn't even taken by me.

text:
Today was terrifying.  One woman came in the dental office and she had to get her tooth pulled because of cavities.  She told us she was scared, but the dentist told her it was going to be fine so I believed him.  She was right to be scared.  It looked excruciatingly painful, and I just stood there in awe and panic and disbelief and nausea and fear.  And then there was blood and I heard the crack when part of the tooth came out.  Teeth look like icebergs.  You see a tiny part and you think you're just pulling out a small little tooth, and then the entire thing comes out and the actual tooth appears to be 3x bigger and longer and bloodier and uglier.

I also have a confession to make.  There was a bag of saltwater taffies in the storage room and I took a handful when no one was looking.
